nodes.label: AI as tutor for learning and understanding
nodes.label_negative: AI as mere translator providing answers without learning
nodes.grounding_span: just using a translator app isn't going to teach you anything, but having a tutor who can answer questions and customize for your needs is really going to help

nodes.label: AI as efficient problem-solver for cyclical tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I spent hours trying to get it right to no avail. Finally I broke down and asked AI ... It wrote a for loop for me ... that solved the problem and I was done so fast

nodes.label: AI as thought partner for growth and new approaches
nodes.label_negative: AI limited to fixing errors only
nodes.grounding_span: I should also use it to grow and think about new ways of doing things. Having that thought partner is probably the best benefit

nodes.label: Reading knowledge of coding languages via AI
nodes.label_negative: Full coding proficiency required without AI
nodes.grounding_span: I could develop a reading knowledge of Python with AI writing code and I understand it without being able to generate it

nodes.label: AI as research assistant (hesitant adoption)
nodes.grounding_span: I'm also trying to be more strategic about AI as a research assistant. This is an area where I like doing research and don't feel like I need help

nodes.label: Openness to AI despite initial fear and pride
nodes.label_negative: Resistance to AI due to fear and pride
nodes.grounding_span: like a lot of people I hadn't used it before out of a combination of fear and pride

nodes.label: Continuous learning and skill development
nodes.grounding_span: I try to figure it out myself and then use AI as my tutor

nodes.label: Professional competence and problem-solving
nodes.grounding_span: It was very satisfying to solve the problem

nodes.label: Personal growth and expanded perspective
nodes.grounding_span: I should also use it to grow and think about new ways of doing things

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI as tutor for coding
nodes.grounding_span: That has really been a gamechanger

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI solving cyclical task
nodes.grounding_span: It was very satisfying to solve the problem and also a 'road to Damascus' moment

nodes.label: Hesitation to use AI for research due to enjoyment
nodes.grounding_span: I have been hesitant to replace something I enjoy with something I would have to fact-check anyway

nodes.label: Openness to AI after overcoming fear
nodes.grounding_span: I realized I needed to be more open to AI

nodes.label: Analogical reasoning — uses language learning analogy to frame AI use
nodes.grounding_span: I try to think about it like studying a foreign language

nodes.label: Strategic skill differentiation — decides which languages to master vs. have reading knowledge
nodes.grounding_span: For R and SQL I want to really learn those, but for Python ... I could develop a reading knowledge
